articles.title: Why China's first submarine missile test in 43 years is ringing nuclear alarm bells
articles.description: China launched a ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine into the Pacific Ocean on Monday, marking its first known submarine-launched ballistic missile test since 1982. The launch showcased the operational readiness of China's sea-based nuclear force, which forms one leg of the country's nuclear triad alongside land-based missiles and air-delivered nuclear weapons.

articles.title: 'Life was enough': Student's final text to boyfriend before body found in Bengaluru lake
articles.description: A 20-year-old undergraduate student was discovered deceased in Sankey Tank on Tuesday morning. Police suspect suicide after the student sent a message indicating intent to end her life. She reportedly entered the lake on Monday evening after leaving college and spending time nearby. Her body was found Tuesday morning after an extensive search by authorities and family. An investigation into the unnatural death is currently underway from all angles.

articles.title: Explained: The VAR decision that left Egypt furious against Argentina
articles.description: Argentina secured a dramatic 3-2 victory against Egypt in the World Cup Round of 16.  Egypt's camp expressed strong dissatisfaction with several officiating decisions during the match.  A disallowed Egyptian goal and the build-up to Argentina's winner fueled their anger.  Coach Hossam Hassan stated his team suffered clear injustice and unfair treatment.  Argentina advanced to the quarter-finals after this controversial and exciting encounter.

articles.title: Is Connor Murphy dead or alive? What we know about the viral rumor surrounding the YouTube bodybuilder
articles.description: Connor Murphy’s reported death remains an unconfirmed rumor as of July 2026. Claims circulating online largely stem from social media posts and statements that have not been independently verified. No family member, representative, or authority has confirmed his death. Murphy rose to fame through fitness and bodybuilding content, and questions about his current status remain unanswered pending official information.

articles.description: Candace Owens criticized the Tyler Robinson hearing as it began in Utah, calling it a "show trial" and questioning the prosecution's evidence. She mocked Charlie Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, and accused her supporters of pushing an emotional narrative. articles.title: Gold price today: Spot gold hits one-week low, slips to $4,100 per ounce
articles.full_text: Gold prices extended their losses, falling to their lowest level in nearly a week as a stronger US dollar and higher crude oil prices dampened investor appetite for the safe-haven metal. Spot gold eased 0.1% to $4,100.32 per ounce by 0107 GMT after touching its weakest level since July 2 earlier in the session, while US gold futures for August delivery dropped 1.1% to $4,112.50 per ounce. The decline came after US strikes on Iran lifted oil prices, fuelling concerns that inflationary pressures could persist. Higher inflation expectations have raised fears that the US Federal Reserve may keep interest rates elevated for longer, reducing the appeal of non-yielding assets such as gold. As a result, investors remained cautious, with bullion coming under pressure despite lingering geopolitical uncertainty. International gold prices also came under pressure, with spot gold falling nearly 0.6% while silver slipped around 2%. Analysts linked the decline to a rebound in crude oil prices following attacks on shipping routes around the Strait of Hormuz. Rising crude prices fuelled concerns over inflation and lifted both US Treasury yields and the dollar index. Since gold is priced in dollars, a stronger greenback makes the metal more expensive for overseas buyers, reducing demand. Higher bond yields further diminished gold's attractiveness because investors could earn better returns from fixed-income assets. These combined factors created fresh pressure on international bullion markets. Market participants largely stayed on the sidelines as they awaited several important economic events from the United States. The release of the Federal Reserve's June meeting minutes, the second round of US-Iran peace talks, ADP employment data, jobless claims and inflation figures were expected to provide fresh direction to gold prices. Investors typically prefer to avoid aggressive positions before such high-impact events because monetary policy expectations directly influence bullion demand. Since gold does not offer interest income, expectations of higher interest rates generally reduce its appeal. Analysts therefore noted that traders adopted a cautious approach, contributing to the weakness in bullion prices as markets waited for greater clarity from upcoming economic data. Gold prices have recorded a steep decline in the domestic market, with the price of 99.9% purity gold falling by Rs 1,400 to Rs 1,49,250 per 10 grams from the previous session. Silver also moved lower, shedding Rs 200 to Rs 2,39,800 per kilogram on Tuesday. Analysts attributed the decline to a combination of a stronger rupee and weakness in global bullion markets. A stronger domestic currency makes imported gold cheaper, thereby reducing local bullion prices. The fall also reflected cautious investor sentiment ahead of key global events that were expected to influence precious metal prices. The decline in both gold and silver highlighted how currency movements can significantly impact domestic bullion even when international fundamentals remain mixed.

articles.title: Stock market today: Will NSE Nifty50 and BSE Sensex shake off Tuesday's losses?
articles.full_text: Global markets opened Wednesday on a cautious note as renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher, while Asian equities showed resilience after an initial volatility. The MSCI Asia Pacific Index was little changed after briefly falling nearly 1% at the open, while US equity futures pointed to a stable start, with S&P 500 futures edging up 0.1% and Nasdaq 100 futures gaining 0.3%. Investors are now watching whether the rise in oil prices translates into broader risk aversion across global financial markets. Brent crude climbed 2.3% to trade near $76 a barrel after the United States launched fresh airstrikes in Iran and revoked a waiver that had allowed Tehran to sell oil globally following attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The developments reignited concerns over potential disruptions to global energy supplies and their impact on inflation. Despite the spike in crude prices, the broader market reaction remained measured. Although benchmark indices ended in the red, domestic market sentiment continued to receive support from steady foreign institutional investor (FII) buying and currency stability. Exchange data showed that foreign investors remained net buyers of Indian equities, purchasing shares worth Rs 393.19 crore during the session. Analysts believe sustained FII inflows, combined with rupee's appreciation, could help cushion the market against external volatility. According to market experts, with geopolitical tensions easing and concerns over trade tariffs receding, investor attention is gradually shifting towards corporate earnings and domestic growth indicators. If earnings remain resilient and foreign flows continue, equities could remain relatively better positioned compared with several global peers despite near-term market fluctuations. Rupee strengthened sharply against the US dollar in the previous session, appreciating 47 paise to settle at 94.96. The currency benefited from improving global risk sentiment as concerns over oil supply disruptions eased following smoother traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Market participants also welcomed Saudi Arabia's decision to reduce crude oil prices for Asian buyers, which is expected to help lower India's import bill and ease inflationary pressures. Forex analysts said the combination of softer crude-related concerns, a weaker dollar and improving investor confidence supported rupee's recovery. A stronger domestic currency also provides comfort to foreign investors and could help stabilise capital flows if global market conditions remain favourable in the coming weeks. Technology stocks emerged as the standout performers in an otherwise weak market, with the Nifty IT index climbing 2.43%. Investors accumulated frontline IT names ahead of the June-quarter earnings season, expecting healthy commentary on demand trends and business outlook after the sector underwent a correction in recent months. Stocks such as HCL Technologies, Tech Mahindra and Infosys figured among the top gainers on the Nifty50, helping cushion losses in the broader indices. Analysts believe investors are positioning themselves ahead of earnings announcements, hoping for signs of improving deal pipelines and resilient revenue growth. The continued strength in IT stocks contrasted sharply with weakness across sectors like realty, metals, pharma and banking, highlighting the market's preference for earnings-driven themes amid global uncertainty. Indian equity benchmarks finished Tuesday's session in negative territory after giving up early gains as investors booked profits in the second half of trade. The BSE Sensex settled 104.35 points lower at 78,180.72, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 31.65 points to close at 24,398.70. Market participants remained cautious ahead of the release of the US Federal Reserve's meeting minutes, which are expected to offer clues on the central bank's interest rate trajectory. Weakness across major Asian markets further dented sentiment during the day. Despite the subdued close, analysts noted that the decline was relatively limited, supported by continued buying in technology stocks and improving foreign institutional investor inflows. Investors are now shifting their focus towards the upcoming earnings season, monsoon progress and global monetary policy signals for fresh market direction.

articles.title: Crude loses cool: Oil prices soar 3% as Middle East tensions flare again
articles.description: Oil prices rose sharply after United States military strikes targeted Iran. These actions followed attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Qatar blamed Iran for striking a liquefied natural gas tanker and an oil supertanker. Maritime security warned of severe threats to ships crossing the Strait. This has reignited fears of further disruptions to vital oil transit routes.

articles.title: Karnataka school holiday: Schools, colleges shut in Hosanagar and Sagar taluks of Shivamogga due to heavy rain
articles.description: Schools and colleges in Hosanagar and Sagar taluks of Karnataka's Shivamogga district will remain closed on July 8 due to heavy rainfall. The holiday, announced as a precautionary measure, also applies to anganwadi centres and PU colleges. Authorities have instructed educational institutions to compensate for the lost working day by conducting classes on holidays. This marks the second school closure in the district since the onset of the monsoon amid continuing adverse weather conditions.

articles.title: Mumbai schools reopen on July 8 as IMD downgrades weather alert; no fresh holiday announced by BMC
articles.description: Mumbai schools are set to reopen on July 8 as the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has not announced any fresh holiday following two days of rain-related closures. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has downgraded Mumbai's weather warning from an Orange Alert to a Yellow Alert, signalling improved conditions. While authorities continue to monitor the monsoon situation, schools, colleges and offices are expected to function normally. Residents have been advised to follow only official updates.

articles.title: UDISE+ 2025-26 report records sharp fall in school dropout rates as teacher strength surpasses 1.02 crore nationwide
articles.description: The UDISE+ 2025-26 report by the Ministry of Education recorded a sharp decline in school dropout rates, with preparatory-level dropouts falling to 1.8% and secondary-level dropouts to 7%. Student retention improved at middle and secondary stages, while showing a marginal decline at foundational and preparatory levels. Secondary gross enrolment ratio rose to 71.7%, and the number of school teachers crossed 1.02 crore for the first time, reflecting sustained progress in India’s education system.

articles.title: ‘Some debts aren’t financial’: Microsoft engineer earning ₹1.9 crore says father sold mother’s jewellery for his education
articles.description: Manu Agrawal's father sold his mother's jewelry to fund his college education. He later achieved significant success in the software engineering field. Agrawal worked for major tech companies like Microsoft and Google. He eventually earned a substantial salary, reflecting his career achievements. This story highlights the profound impact of family support on educational journeys.

articles.title: Karan Johar celebrates PM Narendra Modi’s mention of ‘Kuch Kuch Hota Hai’
articles.description: In Jakarta, Prime Minister Narendra Modi fondly referred to 'Kuch Kuch Hota Hai', calling it a cherished Indian film that resonates well with Indonesian audiences. Karan Johar, who made his directorial debut with the film, took to social media to thank the Prime Minister, feeling proud that his work has achieved such acclaim across borders and touched hearts worldwide.

articles.description: Rumors abound that Kate Winslet is being eyed for the lead in Netflix's upcoming season two of 'Adolescence.' This celebrated drama promises an entirely fresh narrative and cast, building on the first season's triumph, which garnered nine Emmy Awards. Winslet's previous roles in similar narrative realms suggest she could be a perfect fit for this project. However, Netflix has yet to officially announce the new season or confirm her participation.

articles.title: HC issues notice to Salman-linked housing project in Goa
articles.description: Salman Khan received a notice from the Goa High Court regarding alleged CRZ norm violations. A public interest litigation challenges construction at a Candolim property, citing environmental law breaches. The petition claims residential villas were built within a protected 'No Development Zone'. It also questions the validity of an occupancy certificate issued by the local Gram Panchayat. The court has sought responses from various government bodies and the actor.
